title: Glossary v1 (budgeting terms; cited, jurisdiction-flagged)
context: A plain-language glossary of money terms supports learners who encounter unfamiliar terminology in the curriculum lessons. The glossary v1 covers terms introduced in the Budgeting-basics module. Each definition must be cited to an authoritative open source, flagged where jurisdiction-specific, free of product/brand references, and reviewed by the credentialed financial educator. Jurisdiction-specific terms (e.g., account types, regulatory terms) are labeled with their jurisdiction rather than presented as universal.
objective: Write and publish Glossary v1 covering budgeting-related money terms: plain-language definitions, each cited to an authoritative open source, with jurisdiction flags where applicable and notAdviceFraming framing.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers all key terms introduced in the Budgeting-basics module (task 007) with plain-language definitions at or below grade 8 readability
Each definition is cited to at least one authoritative, openly-licensed source from the vetted source registry (task 006)
Jurisdiction-specific terms are clearly labeled with their jurisdiction (ISO country code or 'neutral'); no universal claims for jurisdiction-specific concepts
Linter-clean: no product/brand/affiliate references; persistent 'Educational information, not financial advice' framing where applicable
Glossary schema conforms to the content JSON Schema (task 004): sources[], reviewer, jurisdiction flags, notAdviceFraming, license
Credentialed financial-educator sign-off recorded for the shipped version
output: A reviewed, signed-off Glossary v1 (content/glossary/glossary-v1.mdx or structured data) covering budgeting terms with citations, jurisdiction flags, and conforming metadata.
